fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Delaware CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Delaware - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer CNA license to delaware, delaware CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Delaware.

steps.title: Complete Training Program
steps.description: Complete a state-approved CNA training program (typically 75-120 hours).

steps.title: Pass the State Competency Exam
steps.description: Pass both the written and clinical skills portions of your state's nurse aide competency exam. There is no national certification exam for CNAs.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fees
steps.description: Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary fees.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Delaware CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $30. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Delaware.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: Information on how to become a CNA in DE.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA license to Delaware.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Delaware
hero.dek: Delaware CNA certification requires a 150-hour training program, Delaware DHSS eligibility, and a passing score on the NNAAP (Prometric) exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Delaware | DHCQ Requirements
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to getting your CNA license in DE. Learn about the 150-hour training requirement and Prometric testing.
